title: BUTYRATE
description: Butyrate is a short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) produced by the gut microbiota through the fermentation of dietary fiber, serving as the primary energy source for colonocytes. It acts as a histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor and signals through G-protein coupled receptors to regulate immune responses, maintain intestinal barrier integrity, and stabilize the gut-brain axis.

research.title: Effects of short-chain fatty acid-butyrate supplementation on expression of circadian-clock genes, sleep quality, and inflammation in patients with active ulcerative colitis: a double-blind randomized controlled trial
research.summary: This study found that sodium butyrate supplementation significantly improved sleep quality and reduced inflammatory markers like hs-CRP and fecal calprotectin in patients with ulcerative colitis.

research.title: Efficacy of sodium butyrate adjunct therapy in shigellosis: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial
research.summary: Researchers demonstrated that butyrate enemas improved clinical and histological features of shigellosis by inducing antimicrobial peptides in the intestinal mucosa.

research.title: Calcium butyrate efficacy in pediatric irritable bowel syndrome: Randomized placebo-controlled multiomics-based clinical trial
research.summary: A 2025 clinical trial investigating the efficacy of calcium butyrate in managing pediatric IBS symptoms through microbiome modulation.

research.title: Effect of Supplementation of a Butyrate-Based Formula in Individuals with Liver Steatosis and Metabolic Syndrome: A Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
research.summary: A clinical trial evaluating butyrate's role in improving liver health and metabolic parameters in patients with fatty liver disease.

sentiment.summary: The majority of users view butyrate as a foundational 'biohacking' supplement for gut-driven systemic health, though some report paradoxical effects on sleep and fatigue depending on timing.

effects.label: Gastrointestinal Relief:
effects.description: Highly effective for active ulcerative colitis and IBS, reducing disease severity and abdominal pain.

effects.label: Secondary Metabolic Benefits:
effects.description: Shows promise in reducing liver steatosis and supporting weight loss protocols when paired with diet.

effects.label: Bioavailability Issues:
effects.description: Users note that oral forms must bypass the stomach (tributyrin or enteric coating) to reach the colon effectively.

effects.label: Psychological Adjuvant:
effects.description: Recent evidence suggests it may alleviate psychological symptoms like anxiety and depression associated with gut dysbiosis.

dosage.label: Standard Oral Dose:
dosage.description: Clinical trials often utilize doses ranging from 600mg to 1,200mg of sodium or calcium butyrate daily.

dosage.label: Intervention Loading:
dosage.description: Some users report taking up to 1.2g in the morning to combat bloating and improve digestion.

dosage.label: Topical/Local Application:
dosage.description: In acute infectious cases like shigellosis, 80 mM enemas have been used effectively for localized treatment.

dosage.label: Fiber-First Approach:
dosage.description: Many users prefer 'butyrogenic' diets (inulin, resistant starch) over pills to naturally boost levels.

sideEffects.label: Diurnal Fatigue:
sideEffects.description: Some users report feeling 'unbelievably tired' when taking butyrate in the morning.

sideEffects.label: Sleep Disruption:
sideEffects.description: Paradoxical insomnia or reduced sleep quality reported by some users when taken in the evening.

sideEffects.label: Initial Bloating:
sideEffects.description: Introduction of butyrate or prebiotics can cause temporary gas or 'hunger pangs' as the microbiome shifts.

sideEffects.label: Odor:
sideEffects.description: Sodium butyrate supplements are notorious for a strong, 'cheesy' or rancid smell that many users find unpleasant.

availability.label: Over the Counter:
availability.description: Widely available as a dietary supplement in Sodium, Calcium, and Magnesium salt forms.

availability.label: Tributyrin:
availability.description: Increasingly sold as a more bioavailable liquid fat form that survives digestion better than salts.
